stories.headline: Senate impeachment court to issue pre-trial order June 29 as Sara Duterte trial nears July 6 start
stories.summary: Senate impeachment court spokesperson Reginald Bantug said the pre-trial order in Vice President Sara Duterte's impeachment case will be released on June 29, following a five-day pre-trial conference, and will set the framework for facts, witnesses, and trial dates. The House prosecution has marked witnesses including a sheriff Duterte was accused of punching in 2011 and, according to Manila Rep. Joel Chua, is seeking leeway from the court on how it presents evidence.
stories.context: Duterte faces articles of impeachment covering alleged misuse of confidential funds, false financial disclosures, unexplained wealth, and alleged threats against President Marcos and his family.
stories.watch_for: The pre-trial order is expected June 29, with a comment period until July 1; the trial proper is set to begin July 6, with hearings initially on Mondays to Wednesdays.

stories.headline: Defense department and Chinese embassy trade accusations over South China Sea ahead of arbitral ruling anniversary
stories.summary: The Department of National Defense accused the Chinese Embassy in Manila of insincerity and duplicity after the embassy again called the 2016 arbitration award on the South China Sea illegal and void. The Department of Foreign Affairs reaffirmed the ruling as final and binding and part of international law. The exchange comes as both sides escalate rhetoric over the West Philippine Sea.
stories.context: The 2016 Permanent Court of Arbitration ruling rejected China's expansive nine-dash-line claim. China has never recognized the award.
stories.watch_for: The ruling marks its 10th anniversary on July 12, 2026.

stories.headline: New US ambassador Lee Lipton arrives in Manila
stories.summary: The US Embassy said new US Ambassador to the Philippines Lee Lipton arrived in Manila on June 28 to begin his posting, replacing MaryKay Carlson. Lipton, a Florida businessman appointed by President Trump in October 2025, has no prior career diplomatic experience. The embassy said his priorities include security cooperation, supply chains, trade, and the Luzon Economic Corridor initiative.
stories.context: The arrival comes amid heightened Philippines-China tensions in the West Philippine Sea and continuing US-Philippine defense and economic engagement.

stories.headline: Senate panel to review Juvenile Justice Act, including age of criminal liability, after crimes involving minors
stories.summary: A Senate committee led by Sen. Erwin Tulfo said it will conduct a comprehensive review of how the Juvenile Justice and Welfare Act is being implemented, prompted by recent incidents involving minors, including a shooting at a Tacloban school and stabbings in Negros Occidental and Cavite. The review will examine rehabilitation facilities, parental accountability, intervention programs, and whether the current age of criminal liability remains appropriate.
stories.context: Lowering the minimum age of criminal responsibility has been debated in the Philippines for years. The panel said it will gather input from legal experts, law enforcement, social workers, and affected families.

stories.headline: Government submits proposed P7.2-trillion national budget for 2027
stories.summary: The Department of Budget and Management submitted to Congress a proposed P7.2-trillion national budget for 2027, about P407 billion or 6 percent higher than the 2026 outlay and equal to roughly 21.7 percent of GDP. The framework, signed by acting Secretary Kim Robert de Leon, cited narrow fiscal space due to mandatory spending and projected interest payments of about P950 billion.
stories.watch_for: Congress convenes its second regular session on July 24, when deliberations on the 2027 budget are expected to begin.

stories.headline: Oil industry source projects fuel price increases next week amid Middle East tensions
stories.summary: An oil industry source told GMA News that pump prices could rise next week by up to P2.00 per liter for gasoline and P1.00 per liter for diesel, citing renewed tensions between the United States and Iran. The projected increases would reverse rollbacks implemented days earlier. The figures are estimates and not yet final.
stories.watch_for: Oil companies typically announce final weekly price adjustments on Tuesdays.

stories.headline: WTO review flags high Philippine trade costs as a hurdle to investment
stories.summary: In its sixth Trade Policy Review of the Philippines, the World Trade Organization said high trade costs remain a key obstacle, estimating logistics costs at about 27 percent of retail prices and overall trade costs at roughly 20 percent above the ASEAN average. The WTO urged the country to ease regulatory uncertainty and close infrastructure gaps to attract investors and deepen its role in electronics and semiconductor supply chains, while crediting ongoing digitalization and liberalization reforms.
stories.context: Trade Policy Reviews are periodic WTO assessments of a member's trade regime.

stories.headline: Trade department to recommend lifting 2019 freeze on new Metro Manila economic zones
stories.summary: The Department of Trade and Industry said it will recommend to Malacanang the lifting of Administrative Order No. 18, the 2019 moratorium that halted new PEZA-registered economic zones in Metro Manila to steer development to the provinces. Trade Secretary Ma. Cristina Roque said the department will endorse lifting the order, and PEZA Director-General Tereso Panga said his agency has been cleared to formally endorse it to the Office of the President.
stories.context: PEZA accreditation confers tax and customs incentives sought by IT-BPO and locator firms. Several office buildings in Makati and Taguig are awaiting accreditation.
stories.watch_for: The recommendation now awaits approval by the Office of the President.

stories.headline: Police say anti-corruption White Ribbon March at EDSA was peaceful
stories.summary: The Philippine National Police said the White Ribbon March, an interfaith anti-corruption demonstration, was peaceful and orderly on June 28. The PNP said about 3,600 participants gathered at the People Power Monument along EDSA in Quezon City after a Mass and march from the EDSA Shrine, with nearly 10,000 officers deployed for security and traffic management. Organizers called for accountability and action against corruption and political dynasties.
stories.context: The march was backed by religious and civil society groups, including the Interreligious Leaders Council for National Transformation.

stories.headline: Experts warn pollutants from monthlong Navotas landfill fire could persist for years
stories.summary: Environmental experts warned that pollutants released by a 30-day fire at the Navotas landfill, which began April 10 and burned more than half of its 41-hectare area, could linger in Metro Manila's environment for years and pose long-term health risks. They identified dioxins and furans among the most hazardous pollutants, with possible effects ranging from respiratory irritation to cardiovascular disease and cancer, and urged expanded air-quality and gas monitoring.
stories.context: The fire, which experts said was likely triggered by methane buildup, was extinguished after about 30 days.

stories.headline: General Santos suspends in-person classes after magnitude 6.5 earthquake
stories.summary: General Santos City officials suspended in-person classes at public schools pending structural reassessments after a magnitude 6.5 earthquake centered about 70 kilometers west of Balut Island in Davao Occidental was felt at Intensity V in parts of Mindanao. Phivolcs said the tremor was a separate event, not an aftershock of the magnitude 7.8 quake that struck the region earlier in June. Affected schools shifted to alternative delivery while buildings are inspected.
stories.watch_for: Officials said further class suspensions are possible pending structural assessments.

stories.headline: Four wounded in shootout between police and armed group in Arayat, Pampanga
stories.summary: Police said four people were wounded in a shootout in Arayat, Pampanga, after residents reported indiscriminate gunfire and the armed group opened fire on responding officers rather than surrender. Police said one of the wounded was a former communist rebel who surrendered in 2018 and headed a local armed group. Authorities said they recovered several firearms and a grenade, and all four wounded were hospitalized.

stories.headline: PDEA reports P40 million in drugs seized and 110 arrested in week of operations
stories.summary: The Philippine Drug Enforcement Agency said it conducted 83 anti-drug operations nationwide from June 18 to 25, seizing about P40.06 million worth of illegal drugs and arresting 110 suspects. The agency said seizures included roughly 3,575 grams of shabu and more than 72,000 marijuana plants, and that 76 of those arrested were tagged as pushers and 19 as possessors.

stories.headline: Cagayan's Rina Andrea Delos Santos crowned Miss Philippines Earth 2026
stories.summary: Rina Andrea Delos Santos of Ballesteros, Cagayan, was crowned Miss Philippines Earth 2026 at the pageant's coronation night in Bukidnon on June 27, competing against 31 other candidates under the theme Love in Action. She also won the Miss Hana special award. The pageant named four Elemental Queens during the ceremony.
stories.context: The Miss Philippines Earth winner represents the country in the international Miss Earth pageant.

stories.headline: PAGASA says low-pressure area and monsoon to bring rains over parts of the country
stories.summary: In its 4 p.m. bulletin on June 28, PAGASA said a low-pressure area about 705 kilometers east of Surigao City and an active southwest monsoon would bring moderate to at times heavy rains over several areas. The agency flagged the risk of flash floods and landslides in Eastern Visayas, Caraga, the Davao region, Catanduanes, Albay, and Sorsogon, and monsoon rains over Ilocos, Zambales, Bataan, Occidental Mindoro, and Palawan. Metro Manila could see isolated rainshowers or thunderstorms.
stories.context: PAGASA said no tropical cyclone was inside the Philippine Area of Responsibility as of June 28; the hazard is rainfall from the low-pressure area and the seasonal southwest monsoon.
stories.watch_for: PAGASA reissues its general weather forecast roughly every 12 hours and would begin numbered tropical cyclone bulletins if the low-pressure area develops into a tropical depression.

stories.headline: US and Iran exchange new strikes as Iran targets US bases in Kuwait and Bahrain
stories.summary: Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps said it launched missiles and drones at US installations in Kuwait and Bahrain on June 28, a day after US strikes on Iranian targets near the Strait of Hormuz that Washington said followed a drone attack on a commercial tanker. Both sides accused each other of violating a June interim agreement. Iran's Guards warned the escalation could halt diplomatic efforts to end the conflict.
stories.context: The conflict began with strikes on Iran in February 2026. The Strait of Hormuz carries a large share of seaborne oil, and Asian economies including Japan and South Korea source much of their crude through it.

stories.headline: Venezuela earthquake death toll passes 1,400 as rescuers race to find survivors
stories.summary: Twin earthquakes of magnitudes 7.2 and 7.5 that struck Venezuela on June 25 have killed at least 1,430 people, with tens of thousands reported missing, devastating the coastal La Guaira area near Caracas, according to Al Jazeera. With a shortage of heavy equipment, residents dug through rubble by hand as international rescuers arrived; officials said about 1,600 foreign rescue personnel had reached the country as the critical 72-hour survival window closed.
stories.context: The mainshock is described as Venezuela's strongest in over a century. Caracas's main airport was heavily damaged, and the UN warned that millions may need shelter, water, and medical care.

stories.headline: Heatwave breaks temperature records across Germany, Denmark, and the Czech Republic
stories.summary: Germany set a provisional all-time high of 41.5C on June 27, its second record in two days, as an early summer heatwave moved north and east across Europe, with records also falling in Denmark and the Czech Republic, according to the BBC. The report said an estimated 150 million people in Europe were experiencing temperatures above 35C, and the World Meteorological Organization warned of major impacts on health and ecosystems.
stories.context: The BBC reported the heatwave, which began in the Iberian peninsula, has been linked to hundreds of deaths over the past week.

stories.headline: Burkina Faso severs diplomatic ties with France
stories.summary: Burkina Faso said it was cutting diplomatic ties with France, with its communications minister accusing Paris of ceaseless activism and neo-colonial ambitions against the country. France's foreign ministry called the decision hostile and unfounded and urged its nationals in Burkina Faso to exercise heightened vigilance.
stories.context: Relations deteriorated after Capt. Ibrahim Traore took power in a 2022 coup, expelled French troops, and shifted toward Russia and China, mirroring moves by neighboring Mali.

stories.headline: Ukraine and Russia trade overnight strikes, killing at least three
stories.summary: Overnight Russian drone and bomb attacks killed two people across several Ukrainian regions and injured more than 20, while Ukrainian strikes on Russian and occupied territory killed one and injured 10, according to Al Jazeera. President Volodymyr Zelenskyy said Ukrainian missiles struck an industrial and defense complex in Volgograd; a Russian regional governor confirmed strikes that injured 10 and caused localized fires.
